metadata.summary: Cloud cost management cycle (Inform → Optimize → Operate). FinOps achieves 10-20x ROI by making costs visible, eliminating waste, and optimizing commitments.

content.title: FinOps
content.summary: Cloud Financial Operations: the Inform → Optimize → Operate cycle for cloud cost management. Organizations waste 32% of cloud spend (~$200B/year globally). FinOps teams achieve 10-20x ROI within 30-60 days by making cost visible (tagging), eliminating waste (rightsizing, idle resources), and committing (Savings Plans/Reserved Instances).

why.p: Cloud bills grow unchecked without ownership. FinOps assigns cost accountability to engineering teams via mandatory tags (Environment, Owner, Project, CostCenter), automated anomaly detection, and per-team budget alerts. AI/ML workloads are a special case: GPU costs are 5-10x standard compute, requiring checkpointing + spot instances for training and batching/caching for inference.

list.item: Cloud monthly spend exceeds $10k and cost attribution is unclear
Engineering teams have no visibility into their own cloud costs
Preparing Savings Plan or Reserved Instance purchases (need 30+ days baseline)
Rightsizing instances where avg CPU is below 20% or memory below 30%
AI/ML training workloads where GPU instances run on-demand without checkpointing
Pre-production environments with no steady-state usage patterns — optimization signal is noisy
When cost data is less than 30 days old — too early for commitment recommendations
As a substitute for architecture decisions (e.g., using FinOps to offset a fundamentally overprovisioned design)

section.name: phase-1-inform
section.p: Enable billing exports (AWS CUR, GCP BigQuery, Azure Cost Management) with daily refresh. Target >90% tag coverage for cost attribution.

rule.statement: Enable billing exports (AWS CUR, GCP BigQuery, Azure Cost Management) with daily refresh. Target >90% tag coverage for cost attribution.
rule.rationale: You cannot optimize what you cannot see. 100% tag coverage is unrealistic due to untaggable resources; 90%+ is the mature target.

rule.statement: Mandatory tags: Environment, Owner, Project, CostCenter. Enforce via SCP/Policy to block untagged resource creation.
rule.rationale: Four tags enable attribution by env, person, product, and finance. SCPs prevent drift by requiring tags at creation time, not retroactively.

list.item: Rightsizing — 15-30%
Spot/Preemptible instances — 70-90%
Reserved Instances / Savings Plans — 30-60%
Storage lifecycle policies — 20-40%
Idle resource termination — 10-25%

rule.statement: Purchase Savings Plans only after 30+ days of usage data. Commit to 80% of baseline usage to maintain flexibility.
rule.rationale: Committing to more than baseline usage results in unused commitments (negative ROI). 80% baseline leaves 20% buffer for workload changes.

rule.statement: Set budget alerts at 50%, 80%, 100%, and 120% thresholds. Include a forecasted-overage alert at 100%.
rule.rationale: Multi-threshold alerts give teams early warning (50%) and escalation path (100%+). Forecasted alerts enable proactive action before the month ends.

rule.statement: Conduct weekly cost reviews at team level and monthly reviews with optimization action items and savings tracking.
rule.rationale: Weekly cadence keeps cost accountability active; monthly review measures ROI and adjusts targets.

rule.statement: For training jobs: use Spot instances with checkpoint-based restart. Checkpoint every 100-500 steps to S3/GCS.
rule.rationale: Spot instances cost 70-90% less than on-demand. Checkpointing ensures a spot interruption loses at most one checkpoint interval of work.

example.title: Training loop with S3 checkpointing

rule.statement: For inference: implement response caching (20-60% savings) and request batching. Evaluate model quantization (50% cost, 2x speed).
rule.rationale: Inference at scale is dominated by repeated identical requests and underutilized GPU batch capacity.

section.name: finops-implementation-checklist
section.p: Ordered checklist for implementing FinOps Inform, Optimize, and Operate phases.

rule.statement: Enable detailed billing exports with daily refresh before starting any optimization work.
rule.rationale: Optimization decisions made without current data cause savings estimates to be wrong and commitments to be over-purchased.

list.item: Enable AWS CUR / GCP BigQuery export / Azure Cost Management export
Define mandatory tag schema: Environment, Owner, Project, CostCenter
Implement tag enforcement policies (SCP/Policy) to block untagged launches
Create untagged resource report and assign remediation owners
Enable cloud-native anomaly detection with Slack/email routing
Build team-level cost dashboards with daily/weekly reports
Target >80% tag coverage initially; >90% at mature stage
Run weekly rightsizing analysis: flag instances with avg CPU <20% or memory <30%
Identify idle instances (>7 days <5% CPU): schedule termination or stop
Implement auto-shutdown tags for non-production resources
Analyze 30+ days of usage before purchasing Savings Plans or Reserved Instances
Delete orphaned snapshots, unattached EBS volumes, unused Elastic IPs
Implement S3 lifecycle policies: Standard-IA at 30d, Glacier at 90d, Deep Archive at 365d
For AI/ML training: migrate on-demand GPU to Spot with checkpointing
For AI/ML inference: implement caching and batching before scaling GPU instances

rule.statement: Establish per-team budgets with 50/80/100/120% alerts and a forecasted-overage alert. Review forecast accuracy monthly.
rule.rationale: Teams that never see their costs have no incentive to optimize. Multi-threshold alerts create early warning before budgets are blown.

list.item: Set monthly/quarterly budgets per team and environment
Configure budget alerts at 50%, 80%, 100%, 120% thresholds
Add forecasted-overage alert at 100% (fires before month end)
Conduct monthly cost reviews with action items and savings tracking
Track cost per unit of work (cost per 100k tokens, cost per transaction)
Automate tagging remediation for new untagged resources
Crawl: basic visibility, initial tagging, manual reviews, reactive
Walk: >80% tags, regular reviews, some automation, proactive rightsizing
Run: >90% tags, automated optimization, real-time visibility, cost-aware culture

example.title: Find idle EC2 instances
example.note: Run weekly; pipe output to a Jira ticket or Slack alert for owner assignment.

section.name: savings-plan-calculation
section.p: Example: $100k/month on EC2 on-demand. Usage pattern: 60% steady baseline, 25% predictable peaks, 15% variable.

example.title: Savings plan pricing example
example.note: Use Compute Savings Plans (region-flexible) for baseline, EC2 Instance SP for predictable peaks.

rule.statement: Use AWS Organizations SCPs to require mandatory tags at resource creation time and deny expensive instance families without approval.
rule.rationale: SCPs prevent untagged or oversized resource creation at the API level, eliminating the need for retroactive remediation.

template.purpose: Mandatory tag schema: Environment, Owner, Project, CostCenter with allowed values
Terraform AWS budget with 50/80/100/120% alerts and forecasted overage alert
Monthly cloud cost report template with team breakdown and optimization table
LLM prompt for cost breakdown and rightsizing recommendations
